text: Handling deadlines effectively is a crucial skill in both academic and professional life. Good time management helps you break large projects into smaller, manageable tasks, allowing you to work steadily rather than rushing at the last minute. Prioritization is also important—focus on urgent or high-impact tasks first. However, if a deadline becomes impossible to meet, scope management can help by adjusting the project's requirements or expectations. Above all, open communication with your team or supervisor is key. Letting people know early about potential delays gives everyone time to adjust plans, which reduces stress and builds trust.
